terms.definition: Recording numeric evaluation results over training steps or epochs with split-prefixed names (train/, val/, test/). Canonical definition in this topic.
A time series of one metric value plotted against step or epoch— e.g. val/loss vs epoch used to diagnose learning and overfitting.
The single val/* score used to rank checkpoints and hyperparameter trials— e.g. val/f1 for imbalanced classification.
A metric key that starts with the data split— train/loss, val/f1, test/rmse— so logs from different partitions do not collide.
The optimization loss computed on training batches during fit()— logged frequently to confirm the model is learning.
The same loss function evaluated on the validation split— logged each eval pass for model selection and early stopping.
A monotonic counter of training batches processed— common x-axis for per-step scalar curves alongside epoch-based logging.
A test/* scalar logged once after model selection— e.g. test/f1 tagged as final sign-off, not logged every epoch.
